_comment: Versions at or below these thresholds require legacy_ssh=true (disables rsa-sha2-256/512). Version comparison is semantic — only major.minor checked unless patch matters.

arista_eos.reason: OpenSSH 6.6.1 bundled before EOS 4.25, lacks rsa-sha2-* support

juniper_junos.reason: JunOS < 18.1 uses OpenSSH < 7.2, no rsa-sha2-* pubkey auth

cisco_ios.reason: IOS-XE < 16.9 / classic IOS 15.x SSH server lacks SHA-2 transport
cisco_ios.notes: Classic IOS (12.x, 15.x) always needs legacy. IOS-XE 16.9+ is fine.

cisco_xe.reason: Same as cisco_ios — shared SSH stack

dell_os10.notes: Rare in production, most deployments are 10.5+

nokia_sros.reason: SR OS < 19.10 SSH server doesn't negotiate rsa-sha2-*

linux.reason: OpenSSH version directly — < 7.2 lacks rsa-sha2-* support
linux.notes: Version here is OpenSSH version, not kernel/distro. Debian 9+/Ubuntu 18.04+ ship 7.4+
